Ball-tampering in PSL! Fakhar Zaman caught on camera, Afridi reacts - Watch
description: A ball-tampering controversy has erupted in the Pakistan Super League, centering on Lahore Qalandars' Fakhar Zaman. During a crucial final over, Fakhar was seen altering the ball, leading umpires to award five penalty runs to Karachi Kings.  A disciplinary hearing is underway, with a potential one-match ban if found guilty.

Indian worker killed in Iranian attack on Kuwait power, desalination plants
description: “A service building at a power and water desalination plant was attacked as part of the Iranian aggression against the State of Kuwait, resulting in the death of an Indian worker and significant material damage to the building,” ministry spokesperson Fatima Abbas Jawhar Hayat said. Amid rising tensions, Pakistan said it was willing to facilitate and host “meaningful talks” between Washington and Tehran in an effort to end the month-long US-Israel war with Iran.

Videos show Bengaluru Central Prison inmates using mobiles; 3 cops suspended
description: Disturbing footage from Bengaluru Central Prison has surfaced, revealing undertrials allegedly using mobile devices and pointing fingers at DGP Alok Kumar for the supply. An internal inquiry has indicated that the inmates may have intended to derail ongoing jail reforms. Thankfully, the investigation has cleared DGP Kumar of any wrongdoing, with no substantial evidence found.

IndiGo crew averts disaster as UP man tries to open door mid-air, claims he is ‘possessed’
description: A passenger claiming to be 'possessed' attempted to open an emergency exit door on a Bengaluru-Varanasi IndiGo flight. Crew members swiftly intervened, preventing the man from opening the door on two separate occasions. The pilot temporarily ascended as a precaution before the flight landed safely, and the passenger was detained.

In Census, 'stable' live-in couple to be counted as married
description: Census 2027 will recognize live-in couples in stable unions as married.  The self-enumeration portal clarifies that such relationships should be treated as married. This marks a public acknowledgment, though officials note similar declarations were accepted in past censuses.  The question is part of the houselisting operations.

How a village potter invented a refrigerator that works without electricity
description: A Gujarati potter, Mansukhbhai Prajapati, revolutionized rural India with his electricity-free clay refrigerator. Born from the aftermath of the 2001 earthquake, this innovative device uses evaporation to keep food and water cool for days. Priced affordably, it offers a vital solution for households facing power cuts and limited resources, proving that practical innovation can emerge from traditional skills.

title: Google ex-CEO Eric Schmidt says coding is no longer a key skill
description: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt reveals that top programmers are now writing specifications and evaluation criteria, letting AI handle the coding overnight. This shift, he argues, signifies AI's broader impact on industries by automating mundane business operations and unlocking breakthroughs in fields like medicine and engineering.
